title: quercus ilex f. obesa
intro: Quercus ilex f. obesa, also known as the "Evergreen Oak", is a subspecies of the Holm Oak tree, native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Quercus ilex f. obesa has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Quercus ilex f. obesa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
